articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: Belgium face off with Egypt; Cape Verde shock Spain in draw
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

articles.headline: Spain frustrated by heroic Cape Verde in World Cup opener

images.name: Cape Verde [1296x729]
images.caption: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha impressed against Spain.
images.alt: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha makes a save against Spain.
images.credit: (Photo by Roberto SCHMIDT / AFP via Getty Images,

images.name: Burley: Cape Verde couldn't have given a better account of themselves

articles.headline: Why are there hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup, and why has FIFA been criticised?
articles.description: All 104 matches at the 2026 World Cup are stopping for two hydration breaks. Why have they been stipulated by FIFA, and why have they been criticised?

images.name:
Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.alt: Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium

images.name: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium [1296x729]
images.caption: Argentina's Enzo Fernández said he felt "dizzy" while playing in "very dangerous" temperatures during last summer's Club World Cup in the United States.
images.alt: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium

images.name:
Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Advertising is prominent on stadium screens during drinks breaks at the World Cup.
images.alt: Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium

images.name:
Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Virgil van Dijk has been critical of the hydration breaks introduced at this summer's World Cup.
images.alt: Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium

images.name:
The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Carlo Ancelotti was thankful for the opportunity to speak to his players when Brazil found themselves a goal behind to Morocco in the first half of their World Cup opener.
images.alt: The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium

images.name:
Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Hydration breaks provide a window for coaches to gather their players and tweak their tactics.
images.alt: Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia

images.name:
Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Drinks breaks could have a major effect on the outcome of matches at the World Cup.
images.alt: Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
images.credit: Kirill KUDRYAVTSEV / AFP via Getty Images

competitions.altGameNote: FIFA World Cup, FIFA World Cup

commentary.text: Slovakia winger Vladimir Weiss, son of the manager, believes he must work extra hard to maintain the respect of his team-mates.
Join us at 11.30 GMT to see if Slovakia or Paraguay can go top of the group.
We're at the Free State stadium today, with Paraguay and Slovakia both looking for their first win of the tournament.
For Paraguay, the big news is that Roque Santa Cruz starts, while Slovakia will be looking to Marek Hamsik to open up the Paraguay defence.
The teams are out in Bloemfontein, almost time to get going in this one.
KICK OFF Slovakia get us under way.
It has to be said that the attendance at the Free State stadium is disappointing.
First action, good move from Slovakia, but the final ball finds Sestak in an offside position.
Paraguay get into the Slovakia half for the first time. Barrios whips in a dangerous ball, but fortunately for Slovakia no one is there to get on the end of it.
Santa Cruz hits a wicked drive off Skrtel, Mucha does well to turn it behind. The corner is dealt with and cleared for a Paraguay throw in.
Paraguay put together the first real passing move of the match. Enrique Vera finds space and plays and neat ball into Santa Cruz. He turns and shoots but scuffs it wide.
Slovakia under a little bit of pressure here as Barrios brings it forward down the left and Durica is forced to concede a throw.
Sestak is down after a clash of heads with Bonet. To add insult to injury the free kick is given Paraguay's way.
Chance for Paraguay to get the ball in the box. Skrtel going through the back of Valdez, free kick given.
The free kick cones to nothing and Skrtel can boot the ball down field.
Poor from Durica. Hamsik bursts forward, finds Durica in space and with Sestak and Vittek in the middle he can only find the stands.
Another free kick for Paraguay after another Skrtel foul. Paraguay have started well.
Morel's free kick is again easily cleared by Slovakia.
Valdez and Hamsik take it turns to give away possession. Both teams are struggling to hold onto the ball at the moment.
Third free kick for Paraguay, this time for a foul on Santa Cruz.
Slovakia need to maintain possession and find a rhythm in this game.
Paraguay keeping the ball and Slovakia are having to work really hard to stay with them at the moment.
First hint of an attack from Slovakia. Vladimir Weiss does excellently down the right, he feeds Sestak who is tackled. Much better from them though.
Paraguay have another free kick, the ball is whipped in but play is brought back and the kick is to be retaken.
Da Silva gets in front of Vittek and nearly gets on the end of a great ball in. Goal kick.
Vicious drive from Riveros! Valdez brings the ball forward before laying off to Riveros to hit a rasping 20 yard drive. Unfortunately for him it was straight at Mucha.
Carlos Bonet falls after a challenge.
Paraguay are showing real intent here. They haven't created a clear cut chance yet, but if it carries on like this it's a matter of time until they take the lead.
Chance for Slovakia to build an attack with a throw in the Paraguay half.
Lovely link-up play from Paraguay. Barrios back heels for Vera, who returns the ball to Barrios who fires his effort just over.
Paraguay look to want this more. Valdez turns smartly before hitting his low drive wide.
Slovakia seem content to play for a point here. A dangerous tactic with World Champions Italy to play in their final game.
GOAL A lovely goal by Vera. The impressive Lucas Barrios feeds the ball through to Vera, who finishes smartly with the outside of his right foot. More than deserved for Paraguay.
What have Slovakia got in reply? Paraguay look too good for them here.
Slovakia need to get Weiss in the game. He's looked bright and is always eager to push forward.
This defeat could effectively seal Slovakia's fate. On this form you wouldn't expect them to get anthing out of the Italy game.
Better from Slovakia. Sestak and Vittek combine well. Sestak's cross is too long for Weiss at the far price.
Vittek close to getting on the end of decent ball into the box. Slovakia are trying to up the tempo here.
Alcaraz with a vital interception. The ball is played up towards Sestak, who would've been away if it wasn't for an excellent Alcaraz intervention.
Santa Cruz gets in behind Slovakia, but the through ball to him is over hit. At the other end, Hamsik escapes to get into a crossing position, but he over hits his ball to Sestak.
Corner to Slovakia, a much better contest since the goal.
Salata should've done better! Good delivery from the corner, the centre back finds himself free six yards out, but directs his header over the crossbar.
Stat Attack: Lucas Barrios has scored 3 goals and delivered 2 assists in his first 5 games for Paraguay.
Vladimir Weiss is by far Slovakia's brightest player. He draws three players to him before eventually being tackled.
Santa Cruz should've scored! Kozak with a terrible back pass, the Manchester City man is in and his low effort across goal is saved by the feet of Mucha.
Lucas Barrios is playing really well. He bustles his way down the right, but his cross is over hit and goes out for a goal kick.
Jan Durica goes in the book for going through the back of Riveros.
The free kick only results in a Slovakia free kick after jostling in the penalty area.
Sestak was almost in. A long ball punted up field is just too long and runs through to the greatful Justo Villar.
Enrique Vera goes into the book for a challenge on Jan Durica.
ONE ADDED MINUTE Into injury time at the end of the first half.
HALF TIME Paraguay lead at the break. Their only regret may be that they haven't converted their dominance into a second goal.
Stat Attack: Seven of Paraguay's last nine wins have been by a 1-0 scoreline.
Enrique Vera (right) celebrates.
Can Slovakia get back into this one? On the evidence of the first half you'd doubt it, but stranger things have happened at this World Cup...
Paraguay have been impressive. Valdez and especially Barrios have looked very lively in attack.
SECOND HALF Paraguay restart the game.
Stanislav Sestak goes in the book for a late lunge on Riveros. Dangerous free kick for Paraguay.
Morel's ball in was decent one, the ball is cleared to Alcaraz who earns a throw for Paraguay.
The throw comes to nothing and Slovakia come forward.
Valdez doing excellent defensive working. Peter Pekarik was ready to pull the trigger 20 yards out, but Valdez slid in to block.
Slovakia try and come forward through Weiss, who can only find a Paraguay defender with his through ball. Paraguay look very comfortable.
Weiss again sparks Slovakia into life. He feeds the ball down the line to Sestak, who aims a cross at Vittek who is inches away from connecting with the ball.
Weiss is showing some lovely touches. He's key to Slovakia's hopes of getting back into this.
Vladimir Weiss Snr. must be thinking about making changes. At the moment they don't look like getting back into this.
Slovakia are starting to pass the ball a lot better. Having said that, it's all in front of the Paraguay defence and is being easily dealt with.
First spell of sustained pressure from Slovakia but their still struggling to create chances.
First glimpse of Barrios in the second half. His clipped ball through to Valdez looked perfect until a last gasp intervention.
Vittek does well to control a clearance up field, he turns to run at Alcaraz, who deals with him with ease. Impressive defending.
Corner to Slovakia. A strong run from Durica results in Bonet blocking his low cross. A real chance.
Kozak's corner is a good one, just too high and evades everyone. The ball is eventually worked all the way back to the Slovakian goalkeeper and the chance is gone.
This is too comfortable for Paraguay. Slovakia need to show more urgency.
Santa Cruz is down and looking hurt after a wreckless Skrtel challenge. Not good from the Liverpool man.
Fortunately Santa Cruz is fine to continue as Valdez shows great desire to keep the ball in the Slovakia half.
Nelson Haedo Valdez off for Aureliano Torres
An impressive performance from Valdez, his pace and energy tested Slovakia.
Stanislav Sestak off for Filip Holosko
Sestak has had no impact on this one, he'll be disappointed with how his World Cup has unfolded.
A wonderful move from Paraguay. Santa Cruz is played in down the left and he clips in for Vera to head just wide. Paraguay will be eager to put this one to bed.
A nervy moment for Mucha! He struggles to control a back pass, his eventual clearance cannons off Vera and, luckily for Slovakia, goes out for a throw in.
Vera again coming forward. This time Barrios' through doesn't quite find his way to him. He has looked good all afternoon and deserves his goal.
Torres hits a piledriver from 25 yards, however it's always rising and clears the crossbar.
Slovakia still seem to be sitting back. They need to push forward and take risks or their campaign is as good as over.
Oscar Cardozo on for Lucas Barrios
Not a bad substitute to have is it? Cardozo was prolific for Benfica last season and will fancy his chances here.
Miroslav Stoch on for Kornel Salata
Slovakia showing some intent, an attacker on for a defender.
Vladimir Weiss gets his name taken for arguing the award of a free kick to Paraguay.
GOAL! Cristian Riveros puts this one to bed with a cracking drive. A free kick is played into the box. It is eventually worked back to Riveros who hits an excellent left-foot shot.
Edgar Barreto on for Enrique Vera
A great performance from Vera. As poor as Slovakia have been, you can only beat what's in front of you and Paraguay have done that comfortably.
Slovakia must be fearing the worst now. After this performance they won't be holding out much hope of beating Italy in their final game.
FOUR MINUTES OF ADDED TIME
Vladimir Weiss hits a shot which seemed to be born out of pure anger at his team's performance. Needless to say it didn't trouble Villar's goal.
Slovakia save their best moment till the end. A neat turn and thunderous shot from Vittek is turned over by Villar. Slovakia's first shot on targe in the 92nd minute.
FULL TIME A comfortable 2-0 victory for Paraguay!
Paraguay only need a point against New Zealand to qualify, whilst Slovakia need to beat Italy and hope results go their way to stand any chance of progression.
A very impressive performance from Paraguay. Slovakia have every reason to be disappointed with their two games at this World Cup.
Read our full match report on Slovakia 0-2 Paraguay here
